cycleContext.note: May 29 scan covers the 24-hour window since the May 28 cycle. Six active forward signals from prior cycles. Key new events in window: (1) Hungary Sulyok May 31 deadline has effectively expired as of scan date — constitutional confrontation outcome determinative; (2) Bolivia military deployment now fully operational under May 27 law; (3) Oracle WARN Act last-working-day window opens May 30 — peak litigation exposure; (4) DeepMind CEO Hassabis warned of "species-level transition" with "little margin for error" at Stanford (May 29); (5) Israel: continuing denial pattern documented in OCHA Sit. Update 295 (May 28-June 3 window); (6) UN World Cities Report 2026 launched May 19 Baku — global housing as human right; (7) Meta 8,000 layoffs confirmed May 20; (8) EU AI Act fully applicable August 2, 2026 — 64 days away.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: DEADLINE PASSED (May 31 expired). Sulyok confirmed on May 18 he would not resign. Magyar stated constitutional amendment process would begin after deadline. 16th Amendment (introduced May 20) includes 8-year PM term limit. Constitutional removal mechanism requires 5-day presidential referral window and 30-day court review if procedural challenge filed. Outcome: (a) Sulyok concedes or (b) Magyar proceeds with constitutional amendment — either is an assessable ACC event. HOLD until June 2 for outcome confirmation.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: WARN Act last-working-day window opens May 30
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: WINDOW OPENS TOMORROW. May 30 begins WARN Act effective-date exposure for the March 2026 mass layoffs. Remote-worker classification dispute (algorithm-based WARN avoidance) is the novel legal question. Class action investigations (Strauss Borrelli) in Washington and Missouri. Legal gray area in algorithm-driven firing documented. Monitor through June 15.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: Hold lifted, assessment outstanding since April 18
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: STILL OUTSTANDING. 41 days since April 18 baseline. Frontier Governance Framework (May 28) is the most significant new governance-positive since the for-profit conversion. Assessment package now includes: (1) Frontier Governance Framework, (2) PBC conversion standing post-Musk verdict, (3) mission-safety-word deletion, (4) Kalinowski resignation over military deal, (5) 98-employee + 796 cross-company protest letters, (6) GPT-5.2/5.3/5.4 capability classifications. Comprehensive assessment warranted.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: Military deployment law enacted May 27 — escalation above first-baseline
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: ESCALATING. Military now authorized and deployed under May 27 law. Four deaths confirmed. La Paz at breaking point. No resolution. Crisis in fifth week. IBTimes characterizes as darkest hour. WSWS: government forces clearing protests. Planet News: political crisis deepening. First-baseline 30.9 applied May 26 may need upward-impact reassessment given military deployment escalation.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: Scale-floor annotation pending — methodology ruling required
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: READY FOR ASSESSMENT. Internet partially restored May 25. 88-day shutdown documented. Amnesty confirmed 39 political executions + 6,000+ arbitrary arrests since Feb 28 US-Israel attack. Full assessment package available. Scale-floor at 2.5 — methodology ruling on whether 88-day shutdown + 80% of global executions warrants a distinct methodology annotation.

pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.signal: Floor entity — new OCHA Sit. Update 295 events in window
pendingWatchesFromPriorCycles.status: NEW EVENTS. OCHA Sit. Update covers May 28-June 3 window. 59% aid denial rate on authorized corridor. Family of 9 killed As Saftawi (May 28). 23+ killed Al Bureij (May 29). HRW May 19 report establishes post-ceasefire civilian targeting as documented pattern. Score cannot go below 0, but documentation of authorized-resumption-with-systematic-denial mechanism warrants formal assessment cycle.

prioritizedEntities.name: Afghanistan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Taliban Decree No. 18 (May 14): no minimum marriage age, child marriage only annullable after puberty. Women civil servants terminated without pay (January 2026). 21.9 million (45% of population) require humanitarian assistance. 3 million Afghans returned from neighboring countries in 2025 straining services. Aid cuts worsening crisis. UN World News in Brief (May 26) covers Afghan women's rights. People's Tribunal (Dec 2025) found Taliban guilty of crimes against humanity including gender apartheid.

prioritizedEntities.name: Haiti
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: New UN-backed Gang Suppression Force commander arrived Port-au-Prince May 14. 5.8M Haitians (52%) face crisis-level food insecurity. UN human rights report: 1,243 people killed in 141 drone operations (March 2025-January 2026), including 17 children; 60%+ casualties during anti-gang operations. Sexual violence against women and girls escalating. UN Security Council considering transforming Kenyan-led force into full UN peacekeeping operation.

prioritizedEntities.name: UnitedHealth Group
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: DOJ criminal/civil Medicare fraud probe expanding to Optum Rx physician compensation practices (new scope). CEO Andrew Witty departed abruptly mid-May, replaced by former CEO Hemsley. Securities fraud class action in discovery phase. AI claim denial class action survived motion to dismiss. Change Healthcare data breach settlement discussions. Multiple agencies: DOJ, HHS, SEC, FTC all active. Stock -55% from all-time high. CEO departure under criminal investigation is ACC/INT governance event.

prioritizedEntities.name: Democratic Republic of C
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: UN (May 2026): 26.5 million people facing acute hunger; 3.6 million at emergency levels. New Ebola outbreak (Bundibugyo strain, high lethality) in northeastern Ituri province (Rwampara, Mongwalu, Bunia). 3.59 million internally displaced (South Kivu, North Kivu). Only 23% of those targeted reached under 2026 humanitarian response plan. Goma airport closed disrupting aid access. Rwanda-backed M23 conflict continuing.

prioritizedEntities.name: Sudan
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: UN (May 2026): 20 million facing acute hunger; 800,000 children at risk severe malnutrition. 33.7 million require humanitarian assistance — highest globally. Famine confirmed since Sept 2025 in El Fasher (North Darfur) and Kadugli (South Kordofan). 40% health facilities non-functioning. 9 million displaced. Al Jazeera (May 12): crisis worse than acknowledged. Sudan civil war fourth year (SAF vs RSF since April 2023). Stimson Center: Sudan marginalized in global humanitarian system.

prioritizedEntities.name: Myanmar
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: WFP 2026: Myanmar faces rising displacement and unacceptable hunger. 3.6 million displaced (record high). OHCHR: airstrikes attributed to armed forces killed 982 civilians in 2025 (53% increase YoY), including 287 children. 12 million face acute hunger. Humanitarian response scaled back 27% (from 6.7M to 4.9M target). Amnesty Jan 2026: junta atrocities surge 5 years since coup. UN expert (March 2026): Myanmar at "crossroads" and world must not forsake civilians.

prioritizedEntities.name: Johnson & Johnson
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Bankruptcy court approved $10B+ talc settlement plan Feb 14, 2026 — third bankruptcy attempt succeeded. 62,847 registered claimants as of March. Settlement does not require admission of wrongdoing or liability. Settlement administrator began accepting claims March 1. Prior scan assessment framing of "bankruptcy escape denied" was incorrect — settlement approved Feb 14. Settlement-without-admission methodology question: reparative step vs. accountability avoidance via legal procedure.

prioritizedEntities.name: Anthropic
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Pentagon testing rival AI models to replace Anthropic (Bloomberg May 21). Pentagon maintaining supply chain risk designation while testing 8 competitor models. White House-Pentagon split continues re Anthropic. Federal court injunction holding. EU Commission seeking access to Anthropic cyber models for compliance evaluation (May 12). NYU Stern BHR analysis: Anthropic-Pentagon feud as test case for AI governance. Composite 58.1 is 1.9pt below Established (60.0).

prioritizedEntities.name: Turkey
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Riot police stormed CHP HQ with tear gas and rubber bullets May 24. Court nullified CHP leader Ozel election — HRW: attempt to sideline main political opposition. Multi-city protests in Ankara and Izmir. Ozel march to Parliament May 26. Time Magazine May 25: "Turkiye's Deepening Democratic Crisis." FDD: "decapitation strike" on opposition. Borsa Istanbul fell 6% on May 21 ruling. Wikipedia 2025-2026 Turkish protests actively updating.

prioritizedEntities.name: Bolivia
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Military deployment law enacted May 27 by President Paz. Four deaths confirmed (3 from blocked emergency vehicles including hospital oxygen depletion, 1 in clashes). La Paz at breaking point per Wikipedia 2026 Bolivian protests article. 50+ active roadblocks. IBTimes characterizes as "darkest hour." Four-week general strike with no resolution. US declared humanitarian crisis from blockade-induced shortages of food, fuel, and medicine.

prioritizedEntities.name: Israel
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: OCHA Sit. Update (May 28-June 3): 59% of planned aid movements denied despite nominal authorization via Jordan/Allenby crossing. Family of 9 killed in As Saftawi, North Gaza (May 28). At least 23 Palestinians killed in Al Bureij refugee camp (May 29). HRW (May 19): Israel curbing aid and killing civilians during ceasefire. Cumulative toll 72,619+ confirmed dead through early May. Authorized-resumption-with-systematic-denial pattern is a new conduct sub-category. Post-ceasefire civilian-targeting confirmed as distinct documented pattern.

prioritizedEntities.name: Iran
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Internet partially restored May 25 after 88-day shutdown (longest national internet shutdown on record). Amnesty confirmed 39 political executions and 6,000+ arbitrary arrests since Feb 28 US-Israel attack including journalists, lawyers, and HRDs. Amnesty 2025 annual report: Iran executed at least 2,159 people in 2025 (80% of all documented global executions). OHCHR: imprisoned protesters face expedited executions. Scale-floor methodology ruling required for near-floor composite of 2.5.

prioritizedEntities.name: Amazon
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: Teamsters won first NLRB bargaining order against Amazon (April 2026 — Staten Island ALU-IBT1). Amazon conceded workers' right to strike (May 2026 NLRB mediation settlement). 30,000 roles cut in last 5 months while $725B committed to AI infrastructure. Algorithm-based layoffs drawing legal scrutiny (NELP, CFO.com). Nation investigation: Amazon union-busting to "new heights." Multiple ongoing DSP driver organizing campaigns (CA, GA, IL, NY). Boston Globe Bellingham investigation documented (May 20). SDNY criminal probe ongoing.

prioritizedEntities.name: Microsoft
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: EFF + Amnesty International + Access Now + Fight for the Future + 7amleh joint letter to Microsoft (May 7): release investigation findings, suspend relationships tied to serious human rights abuses, implement safeguards re Gaza. Microsoft Israel chief departed amid ethical controversy over AI/cloud use in military targeting. Company has not fully disclosed scope of investigation. UN Human Rights Council report lists Microsoft among tech companies profiting from Gaza operations. Investor Advocates for Social Justice: investor pressure positively shifted Microsoft on human rights but gaps remain among peers.

prioritizedEntities.name: Oracle Corporation
prioritizedEntities.news_summary: WARN Act last-working-day window opens May 30 — peak litigation exposure period. Multiple class action investigations (Strauss Borrelli: Washington, Missouri). Remote-worker WARN classification dispute (algorithm designating workers "remote" to reduce WARN obligations). Time (Apr 30): workers say fired after training AI to replace them. CFO.com: legal gray area in algorithm-driven firing. 30,000 employees terminated March 2026. Composite 20.6, 0.6pt above Critical boundary.

sectorAlerts.description: Fortune 500 DEI disclosures fell 65% in 2026 per HRC Corporate Equality Index. 144,832 tech workers laid off in Q1 2026 (45-55% of all 2025 layoffs in one quarter). $725B AI infrastructure investment by four companies (Amazon, Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta) replacing worker roles. Structural sector-wide shift away from equity commitments. Affects composite scoring potential for multiple Fortune 500 entities at next assessment cycle.
sectorAlerts.entities_potentially_affected: Multiple Fortune 500 entities with DEI commitments previously scored

sectorAlerts.description: Cross-sector AI ethics event: 98 OpenAI employees + 796 Google employees signed cross-company letter protesting military AI deployments. OpenAI robotics chief Kalinowski resigned March 7. Anthropic maintaining position against "all lawful purposes" military deployment while facing Pentagon blacklist. Google employee backlash (Fortune May 4). Anduril-Palantir consortium formed. This represents the most significant AI sector ethics-vs-military tension since Project Maven (2018). Affects AI Labs sector composite framing.
sectorAlerts.entities_potentially_affected: openai, anthropic, meta-ai, deepmind-google, microsoft-ai, anduril, palantir-ai

sectorAlerts.description: UN-Habitat World Cities Report 2026 launched May 19 Baku at 13th World Urban Forum: 1.1 billion living in informal settlements, 300 million experiencing absolute homelessness, global housing deficit 288M units. Report frames housing as human right and calls on governments to recommit. Affects scoring context for global cities and national government entities with housing dimensions.
sectorAlerts.entities_potentially_affected: All global-cities entities; countries with housing/homelessness scoring dimensions

sectorAlerts.description: EU AI Act full applicability: August 2, 2026 (64 days from scan date). EU Commission seeking direct access to OpenAI and Anthropic cyber models. All frontier model providers classified as systemic risk subject to up to EUR 35M or 7% global turnover penalties. Transparency rules effective August 2026. Compliance teams established at Anthropic, OpenAI, Google. Affects AI Labs sector across the board.
sectorAlerts.entities_potentially_affected: All ai-labs entities with EU market presence

sectorAlerts.description: As of May 2026: 18 deaths in ICE detention in 2026 (one every 6 days), 22-year record high. Detention population at 68,000+ (70%+ increase). AI surveillance tools (Clearview $3.75M, Palantir ImmigrationOS $30M) deployed. Senate Democrats demanded documents from DHS. California state investigation documented 6 deaths in state ICE facilities. UN rights chief decried treatment. Structural harm event affecting: United States, Clearview AI, Palantir AI, potentially Optum/UnitedHealth (detention healthcare provider).

Three simultaneous mega-crises each exceeding prior thresholds: (1) Sudan: 33.7M requiring assistance, famine confirmed; (2) DRC: 26.5M acute hunger + new Ebola outbreak; (3) Myanmar: 12M acute hunger, 982 civilians killed by airstrikes in 2025. Combined: ~72M people in acute need across three countries. All three are in active conflict with state actor perpetrators. Global humanitarian system capacity severely strained (Global Humanitarian Overview 2026).

methodologyNoveCandidates: AUTHORIZED-RESUMPTION-WITH-SYSTEMATIC-DENIAL: Aid nominally authorized but 59% denied in execution (Israel/Gaza OCHA Sit. Update 295)
EXTRATERRITORIAL-CIVIC-DEATH-LAW: State extending civil rights deprivation to diaspora critics via domestic legislation (Russia Civic Death Law, passed Duma May 26)
CONSTITUTIONAL-AMENDMENT-TO-REMOVE-LEGACY-EXECUTIVE-OBSTACLE: PM using supermajority to remove president installed by predecessor regime (Hungary, Magyar vs Sulyok)
ALGORITHM-BASED-WORKER-CLASSIFICATION-TO-AVOID-LABOR-PROTECTIONS: Designating workers "remote" by algorithm to reduce WARN Act obligations (Oracle, WARN Act window)
NATIONWIDE-INTERNET-BLACKOUT-AS-COVER-FOR-MASS-REPRESSION: 88-day internet shutdown enabling 6,000+ arbitrary arrests and 39 political executions (Iran)
GENDER-APARTHEID-FORMAL-RECOGNITION: People's Tribunal Dec 2025 finding Taliban guilty of crimes against humanity including gender apartheid — affects scoring framework for Afghanistan

Settlement-without-admission methodology question: reparative step vs. accountability avoidance via legal procedure.

Boston Globe Bellingham investigation documented (May 20). SDNY criminal probe ongoing.

entity_reviews.name: Venezuela
entity_reviews.summary: Venezuela freed longest-held political prisoners after 23 years (May 19). HRW (May 13): Amnesty law excludes many arbitrarily detained people, applied in ways that may deny eligible prisoners release. UN Fact-Finding Mission: 87 politically motivated arrests since January 3. Revolving door effect confirmed. Political prisoner population above 1,000. HRW May 13 report establishes new evidence package for assessment.

entity_reviews.name: Haiti
entity_reviews.summary: New UN-backed Gang Suppression Force commander arrived Port-au-Prince May 14. 5.8M Haitians (52%) face crisis-level food insecurity. UN human rights report: 1,243 people killed in 141 drone operations (March 2025-January 2026), including 17 children; 60%+ casualties during anti-gang operations. Sexual violence against women and girls escalating. UN Security Council considering transforming Kenyan-led force into full UN peacekeeping operation.

entity_reviews.name: Democratic Republic of C
entity_reviews.summary: UN (May 2026): 26.5 million people facing acute hunger; 3.6 million at emergency levels. New Ebola outbreak (Bundibugyo strain, high lethality) in northeastern Ituri province (Rwampara, Mongwalu, Bunia). 3.59 million internally displaced (South Kivu, North Kivu). Only 23% of those targeted reached under 2026 humanitarian response plan. Goma airport closed disrupting aid access. Rwanda-backed M23 conflict continuing.

entity_reviews.name: Sudan
entity_reviews.summary: UN (May 2026): 20 million facing acute hunger; 800,000 children at risk severe malnutrition. 33.7 million require humanitarian assistance — highest globally. Famine confirmed since Sept 2025 in El Fasher (North Darfur) and Kadugli (South Kordofan). 40% health facilities non-functioning. 9 million displaced. Al Jazeera (May 12): crisis worse than acknowledged. Sudan civil war fourth year (SAF vs RSF since April 2023). Stimson Center: Sudan marginalized in global humanitarian system.

entity_reviews.name: Myanmar
entity_reviews.summary: WFP 2026: Myanmar faces rising displacement and unacceptable hunger. 3.6 million displaced (record high). OHCHR: airstrikes attributed to armed forces killed 982 civilians in 2025 (53% increase YoY), including 287 children. 12 million face acute hunger. Humanitarian response scaled back 27% (from 6.7M to 4.9M target). Amnesty Jan 2026: junta atrocities surge 5 years since coup. UN expert (March 2026): Myanmar at "crossroads" and world must not forsake civilians.

entity_reviews.name: United States
entity_reviews.summary: ICE detention deaths: 18 deaths in 2026 through May 1 (one every 6 days rate), 22-year record high. Detention population 68,000+ (70%+ increase). California investigation documents 6 deaths in state ICE facilities with medical care collapse. Senate Democrats demand DHS documents. Warehouse detention expanding to 96,600 capacity. UN rights chief decries US treatment of migrants. ICE AI surveillance tools (Palantir ImmigrationOS $30M, Clearview AI $3.75M) deployed for tracking. Composite 23.4, boundary at 20.0.

entity_reviews.name: Bolivia
entity_reviews.summary: Military deployment law enacted May 27 by President Paz. Four deaths confirmed (3 from blocked emergency vehicles including hospital oxygen depletion, 1 in clashes). La Paz at breaking point per Wikipedia 2026 Bolivian protests article. 50+ active roadblocks. IBTimes characterizes as "darkest hour." Four-week general strike with no resolution. US declared humanitarian crisis from blockade-induced shortages of food, fuel, and medicine.

entity_reviews.name: Israel
entity_reviews.summary: OCHA Sit. Update (May 28-June 3): 59% of planned aid movements denied despite nominal authorization via Jordan/Allenby crossing. Family of 9 killed in As Saftawi, North Gaza (May 28). At least 23 Palestinians killed in Al Bureij refugee camp (May 29). HRW (May 19): Israel curbing aid and killing civilians during ceasefire. Cumulative toll 72,619+ confirmed dead through early May. Authorized-resumption-with-systematic-denial pattern is a new conduct sub-category. Post-ceasefire civilian-targeting confirmed as distinct documented pattern.

entity_reviews.name: Hungary
entity_reviews.summary: Sulyok May 31 resignation deadline expired. Sulyok confirmed refusal (May 18: "June 1 will come"). Magyar has threatened constitutional amendment to remove him using 2/3 parliamentary majority. 16th Amendment introduced May 20 including 8-year PM term limit. Magyar stated removal legal framework would begin after deadline. EU Commissioner McGrath confirmed Hungary on "very clear and steadfast" rule-of-law path (May 28). Constitutional confrontation outcome now determinative for next assessment cycle.

entity_reviews.name: Iran
entity_reviews.summary: Internet partially restored May 25 after 88-day shutdown (longest national internet shutdown on record). Amnesty confirmed 39 political executions and 6,000+ arbitrary arrests since Feb 28 US-Israel attack including journalists, lawyers, and HRDs. Amnesty 2025 annual report: Iran executed at least 2,159 people in 2025 (80% of all documented global executions). OHCHR: imprisoned protesters face expedited executions. Scale-floor methodology ruling required for near-floor composite of 2.5.

entity_reviews.name: Russia
entity_reviews.summary: Civic Death Law passed Duma May 26 (second and third readings): asset seizure, passport denial, digital service ban for emigrated critics. HRW characterized as extraterritorial civil-rights deprivation via law. Affects estimated 1M+ emigrated Russians. Mediazona/Meduza: 352,000 Russian military deaths over four years in Ukraine war (new estimate). UN Security Council briefing on human cost of Ukraine war (May 26). New methodology sub-anchor candidate: EXTRATERRITORIAL-CIVIL-DEATH-LAW.

entity_reviews.name: North Korea
entity_reviews.summary: UN High Commissioner for Human Rights warned (May 2026) that abuses in DPRK remain a "human rights crisis" requiring sustained international attention. HRW: unrelenting repression, hunger, inequality. Ongoing gross violations, some amounting to crimes against humanity. Severe restrictions on expression/movement. Military prioritized at expense of social services. Hunger and inequality intensifying. South Korea co-sponsored UN rights resolution on North Korea (April 2026).

entity_reviews.name: OpenAI
entity_reviews.summary: Frontier Governance Framework published May 28 covering cyber, CBRN, manipulation, control-loss risks — governance-positive signal aligned with EU AI Act Code of Practice. GPT-5.2/5.3/5.4 cyber capability classifications published. Global AI governance body proposal (US-China led, VP Global Affairs). Concurrent negatives: "safely" mission deletion confirmed structural; robotics chief Kalinowski resigned March 7 over Pentagon surveillance/lethal-autonomy concerns. 98 OpenAI employees + 796 Google employees signed cross-company protest letter. Assessment outstanding since April 18 (41+ days).
